identity.title: Unlaminated Plastics Film and Sheet (except Packaging) Manufacturing

lens.included_activities: US lower-middle-market manufacturers that repeatedly convert plastic resins into nonpackaging unlaminated film or sheet for external industrial, construction, environmental-containment, agricultural, energy, medical, graphics, or downstream fabrication customers.
lens.excluded_activities: Captive internal production, pure resin or film distribution, packaging film and sheet, laminated nonpackaging sheet, profile shapes, finished bags and pouches, shells, and non-control financing vehicles.
lens.customer_and_revenue_model: Recurring rolls or sheets sold by weight, area, gauge, formulation, performance specification, qualification, or supply agreement; resin-index and commodity pricing coexist with differentiated technical products such as liners, specialty films, and energy-related components.

a.bridge: BLS OEWS describes occupations for broader plastics-product manufacturing, not wage-weighted AI exposure in 326113. The estimate maps extrusion, forming, inspection, engineering, maintenance, logistics, sales, and office work to exposed planning, quality-analytics, predictive-maintenance, specification, quoting, procurement, service, and administrative tasks. It may overstate exposure for highly continuous commodity sheet plants or understate it for engineered films with laboratory and technical-service intensity.
a.rationale: Extrusion, calendering, winding, material handling, setup, sampling, and maintenance are physical and often already controlled by automation. A minority of wage cost remains exposed through information-heavy exception handling, quality, engineering, planning, and commercial work.
a.change_evidence: A 326113-specific staffing and payroll matrix paired with task-level time studies at extrusion and calendering plants, documenting current controls and work in operations, quality, laboratory, maintenance, planning, engineering, and customer support.
caveats: The occupation proxy is four-digit and includes many plastics processes outside film and sheet.
Employment counts are not direct task or wage shares.

rho.rationale: Schedule optimization, process anomaly detection, vision inspection, predictive-maintenance support, technical-document retrieval, quoting, and back-office copilots can be implemented within five years. Legacy equipment, scarce labeled defect data, material variability, validation, safety, downtime risk, cybersecurity, and the need for accountable setup and release decisions constrain realized labor avoidance.
rho.change_evidence: Twelve-month before-and-after operating studies from LMM nonpackaging film and sheet plants showing labor hours, scrap, line speed, downtime, quality escapes, complaints, and maintenance outcomes for each deployed workflow.
caveats: The sources describe occupations and automation context rather than observed AI implementation.
Some gains would improve yield or throughput instead of reducing labor.

e.rationale: Independent specialty producers can have repeat, specification-based external relationships and transferable assets. Eligibility is reduced by captive or integrated capacity, large-company ownership, capital intensity, commodity exposure, aging lines, environmental diligence, customer concentration, and products dependent on certifications or founder-held application knowledge.
e.change_evidence: A verified firm and plant census with ownership, EBITDA, recurring external revenue, product/end-market mix, customer concentration, line age and utilization, permits, certifications, technical IP, maintenance capital, and transferability of supply approvals.
caveats: The injected firm count is model-derived.
The code combines films and sheets serving unrelated end markets, so plant-level eligibility cannot be inferred from the label.

s5.bridge: Census ABS owner-age data cover employer businesses broadly rather than qualifying five-year control transfers of 326113 LMM manufacturers. The range translates general succession exposure and discounts internal succession, closures, corporate reorganizations, failed deals, and plants without transferable economics. It may overstate a consolidated capital-intensive sector or understate mature founder-led specialty producers.
s5.rationale: General succession pressure and a niche independent-producer population support some control flow, but owner age is not deal evidence and no comprehensive six-digit transaction series was found.
s5.change_evidence: Five years of PitchBook, Capital IQ, GF Data, Axial, broker, bankruptcy, and asset-sale records reconciled to a 326113 roster and classified by control, EBITDA band, operating continuity, and buyer type.
caveats: ABS contains industries and sizes outside the lens.
A line or plant asset sale may not transfer a complete recurring operating firm.

q.rationale: Fixed-price periods and qualified or engineered performance can preserve savings in liners, solar components, medical, graphics, or specialty industrial film. Resin-index clauses, transparent conversion economics, competitive bids, customer concentration, and substitutable commodity sheet can pass benefits through at renewal.
q.change_evidence: Contract and invoice cohorts by product family covering resin and energy indices, conversion spreads, open-book terms, rebates, bid cadence, customer concentration, qualification costs, and price realization after documented productivity gains.
caveats: No source directly measures five-year retention of AI-derived benefit.
Bargaining power differs substantially across commodity construction sheet and highly specified environmental or energy film.

d5.bridge: Census construction spending, EPA geomembrane guidance, and DOE solar-component information measure downstream markets and technical uses rather than constant-quality 326113 shipment volume. Construction and environmental containment provide a mature base, while solar encapsulant and backsheets offer growth; resin substitution, lightweighting, recycling policy, imports, and cyclical construction create downside. Nominal construction spending can overstate physical demand under inflation, while selected high-growth solar uses may overstate their weight in the total code.
d5.rationale: Nonpackaging film and sheet serves diverse durable applications, including regulated containment and energy components, so it is not exposed to the same direct single-use packaging reduction pressure as adjacent codes. Current construction is soft, but environmental, infrastructure, and energy uses support a modest-growth base with substantial product-mix uncertainty.
d5.change_evidence: Census product-class shipment tonnage by resin and end use, customer-industry forecasts for construction sheet, geomembranes, agriculture, medical, graphics, and solar films, plus import penetration and substitution data.
caveats: Construction spending is nominal and only one end market.
EPA liner sources demonstrate required applications but not current shipment growth.
DOE solar evidence does not establish the domestic share supplied under this NAICS code.

o.rationale: Remaining demand for physical film or sheet requires resin conversion, gauge and property control, winding or cutting, testing, release, and delivery. Software can optimize specifications or reduce material use but cannot itself provide a liner, encapsulant, protective sheet, or industrial film. Imports and vertical integration are more plausible operator displacement channels.
o.change_evidence: Customer sourcing and engineering roadmaps that identify volumes eliminated, downgauged, shifted to coatings or nonplastic materials, imported, or vertically integrated, plus evidence of customer-site autonomous extrusion.
caveats: This is conditional on year-five physical demand after substitution and material reduction.
An external lens operator can be displaced even when the end use still requires film.

narrative.executive_view: Nonpackaging plastic film and sheet is a capital-intensive, heterogeneous physical-products industry. AI can improve planning, quality and process analytics, maintenance, specification work, and administration, but does not replace extrusion, calendering, handling, or accountable release. Diverse construction, containment, industrial, and energy uses support demand while making any aggregate forecast uncertain.
narrative.ai_impact: The feasible opportunity lies around the line: recipe and specification retrieval, scheduling, anomaly detection, vision inspection, predictive-maintenance support, laboratory-data analysis, quoting, procurement, and customer service. Physical changeovers, material handling, machine tending, maintenance, sampling, and final quality accountability persist.
narrative.value_capture: Engineered performance, certifications, installation risk, and customer qualification can protect savings in specialty films. Commodity sheet is more exposed to resin indices, rebids, imported alternatives, and customers that understand conversion economics.
narrative.firm_availability: Transferable specialty independents likely exist, but the nominal pool requires filtering for captive integration, large-company ownership, utilization, equipment age, environmental obligations, certifications, customer concentration, and maintenance capital. Broad owner-age data provide only weak succession evidence.
narrative.demand_durability: Construction and industrial cycles matter, while containment requirements and solar or other advanced applications provide durable or growing niches. Lightweighting, alternative materials, imports, and policy pressure on plastics can reduce tonnage, though packaging-specific restrictions are less directly relevant here.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: Major risks include resin and energy volatility, capital intensity, line downtime, thickness and property defects, environmental permits, imported competition, end-market concentration, certifications, and rapidly changing materials. Public sources do not reveal the code's current product weights.
narrative.principal_driver: Specification-critical physical films serving containment, industrial, construction, and energy uses can support recurring demand and operational data advantages.
narrative.principal_weakness: Product-mix opacity and capital-intensive commodity economics make transferable-firm quality and benefit retention highly firm-specific.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Definition: Unlaminated Plastics Film and Sheet (except Packaging) Manufacturing
sources.supports: Defines 326113 as converting plastics resins into nonpackaging film and unlaminated sheet and distinguishes adjacent packaging and laminated activities.

sources.title: May 2023 OEWS: Plastics Product Manufacturing
sources.supports: Provides broader plastics-product occupation counts for extrusion, forming, inspection, engineering, maintenance-adjacent, and other roles used as the task-mix proxy.

sources.title: Metal and Plastic Machine Workers
sources.supports: Describes machine setup, test runs, adjustments, inspection, monitoring, minor repairs, and the effect of automation on multi-machine operation.

sources.title: ABS Characteristics of Business Owners: 2022 Tables
sources.supports: Provides age-of-owner tables used solely as a broad succession proxy.

sources.title: Monthly Construction Spending: May 2026
sources.supports: Reports May 2026 US construction spending at a $2,210.2 billion annual rate, 1.5% below a year earlier, as a downstream-demand proxy.

sources.title: Materials Management: State of the Practice
sources.supports: Describes plastic geomembrane use in landfill liner systems and regulatory containment requirements, supporting a durable end-use example.

sources.title: Solar Manufacturing
sources.supports: Identifies encapsulant and backsheets among physical photovoltaic module components and describes current domestic manufacturing context.

sources.title: Solar Photovoltaics Supply Chain Deep Dive Assessment
sources.supports: Explains that front and back encapsulant films protect photovoltaic cells and identifies EVA and POE as predominant resins.
